events.title: Gender and Identity in Ancient Egypt
events.description: Explore how the people of ancient Egypt expressed ideas of identity, gender, and sexuality while viewing rarely-seen objects from the Field’s anthropological collections, including jewelry and grooming materials, amulets and objects used in ritual, and funerary decorations. Hear how these objects connect to ongoing research on gender identity, concepti

events.title: ¡Tocamos! Oaxacan Folk Art by Avelino Pérez
events.description: The Field Museum is proud to showcase new sculptures from the collection by Oaxacan artist Avelino Pérez. These beautifully carved and painted figures were created in collaboration with the bilingual children's book author, Cynthia Weill, for her latest publication ¡Tocamos! View our newest acquisitions from Oaxaca and learn about the Field Muse

events.title: PlayLab PlayDate: Juneteenth Celebration
events.description: Families with children ages 2–6 are invited to a free, drop-in PlayDate to celebrate Juneteenth. This PlayDate will feature stories and a craft celebrating the liberation of enslaved Black Americans. Join us for a fun-filled PlayDate with stories and more!

events.title: The Academic Legacy of Berthold Laufer: A Commemorative Symposium
events.description: The Academic Legacy of Berthold Laufer: A Commemorative Symposium children family kids museum science art workshop

events.title: Drawing Inspiration: Art in the Gardens
events.description: Everyone is invited to come observe and participate in Art in the Gardens! Field Museum staff and volunteers who dabble in the arts professionally or for fun will be scattered throughout Rice Native Gardens – the Museum’s largest and free outdoor exhibit, creating art inspired by the gardens in a variety of mediums. Wander through to observe and

events.title: Illinois Free Wednesday

events.title: Nectar Feeders vs. Insect Hunters: A Closer Look at Bats
events.description: Celebrate Pollinator Week with scientists from the Gantz Family Collections Center! Bats are often celebrated as important nighttime pollinators, but not all bats play that role. At this special session, you’ll learn about the fascinating diversity of bat diets and adaptations around the globe. Even though Chicagoland’s bats do not pollinate our

events.title: Field Favorites in ASL
events.description: Field Favorites in ASL children family kids museum science art workshop

events.title: What Insects Call Our Garden Home (and Maybe Yours Too!)
events.description: Feel empowered to be a scientist! Check out equipment scientists use in the field to observe and document insects and see photos and specimens from the insect collections. Discover how even the smaller things in nature are important to biodiversity and learn ways you can document your own observations of nature. Aster Hasle, Lead Conservation Ecologist, and

events.title: Pinned, Preserved, and Powerful: How Collections Help Us Do Science
events.description: Educators, join us for Pollinator Week and explore how scientific collections support conservation, biodiversity research, and the process of doing science. Designed for 6–8th grade educators, this hands-on workshop features pollinating insects, specimen mounting and preservation practices, and inquiry-driven strategies that highlight how collections p

events.title: A Day in the Life of a Pathologist: The Science That Shapes Health
events.description: Visit the Grainger Science Hub and meet scientists from the College of American Pathologists to explore the role of a pathologist and examine its impact on overall health. This interactive drop-in experience will introduce the world of pathology through hands-on observation, pattern recognition, and diagnostic thinking. Visitors will rotate through stations

events.title: ¡Tocamos! A Mexican & American Picture Book and Artisanal Collaboration
events.description: Cynthia Weill is a renowned author of many award-winning bilingual children's books in English and Spanish. These books for young children, known as the First Concepts in Mexican Folk Art series (Cinco Puntos Press, an imprint of Lee & Low Publishers) utilize hand-crafted figures made by artisans from the state of Oaxaca in México. Beginning in 2003,

events.title: Sueños de una Monarca Documentary Showing
events.description: In the short documentary, Sueños De Una Monarca (Dreams of a Monarch), Director Julián Trejo Bax follows environmental activist Claudia Galeno-Sánchez as she turns her Chicago home into a monarch butterfly sanctuary and symbol of hope. After the showing, Julian and Claudia will speak on a facilitated panel about the significance of this

events.title: Dust from the Cosmos
events.description: Visit the Grainger Science Hub to see micrometeorites found on the roof of the Field Museum! Explore how cosmic dust can be found everywhere - from the rooftops of buildings to the deserts of Antarctica. Touch meteorites that are 4.5 billion years old and observe how a Scanning Electron Microscope can be used to identify micrometeorites. This session will be
